qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: DOMParser deep nesting triggers stack overflow during unconditional document normalization
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: this finding is about unbounded recursive `Node.normalize()` after parsing, not `createComment()` data being serialized unsafely by `XMLSerializer`.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: hasOwnProperty token collisions break internal lookup maps and cause DOM API denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: this finding concerns attacker-controlled keys corrupting plain-object lookup maps in `dom.js` and `sax.js`, not comment serialization in `XMLSerializer`.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: XMLSerializer comment and processing-instruction serialization permits XML injection
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The comment subpath matches the GHSA exactly: attacker-controlled data enters `createComment()`, is stored unchanged, and the `COMMENT_NODE` serializer emits it verbatim so `-->` breaks out into live XML. The report is broader because it also includes PI cases, but its comment path independently identifies the advisory root cause and patched sink.

selected_finding.title: XMLSerializer comment and processing-instruction serialization permits XML injection

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding reaches the same `createComment()` to `COMMENT_NODE` serialization path fixed by GHSA-j759-j44w-7fr8, and the copied PoC on xmldom 0.8.12 showed attacker-controlled comment data becoming a real injected XML element after serialization and reparsing.
evidence: The advisory and fix commits b397540889086da868c30c366ad5c220d1a750c7 and fda7cc313de30243fea35cada64e0bb12099c2a1 both patch unsafe comment serialization in `lib/dom.js`, not a different parser or lookup-map bug.
In vulnerable 0.8.12, `createComment()` stores attacker-controlled data unchanged and the `COMMENT_NODE` branch serializes it as `<!--` + `node.data` + `-->` with no well-formedness guard.
The copied PoC run log contains `COMMENT_XML=<root><!----><injected from="comment"/><!----></root>`.
The copied PoC run log contains `COMMENT_INJECTED_COUNT=1` and `COMMENT_INJECTION_CONFIRMED`, proving the advisory-described comment breakout produced a live injected XML node.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC cloned xmldom tag 0.8.12, serialized attacker-controlled comment data containing `-->`, and reparsed output containing a live `<injected from="comment"/>` element (`COMMENT_INJECTED_COUNT=1`).
